bde55575:
> Writing documentation is really a bit difficult for me...  
> If you find anything hard to understand, please don't scold me first (whispering). You can submit an Issue / PR to help me improve it, and I'll be super grateful! Sorry, sorry, sorry (bow.gif)
f2bc8c94: Kecare is a generator framework for modern static blogs and note sites. Its primary goal is not to "create another Hexo," but to separate the **content system** from the **theme/framework**, turning your blogging capabilities into "pluggable modules" meow~
24b2929f: ## What Makes Kecare More "Advanced" Than Hexo: Non-Invasive Meow!
d9b2c291:
Many traditional blogging solutions (like Hexo) will lock you into a fixed ecosystem:  
themes, builds, plugins, directory structures—many things are tightly coupled with it. If you want to switch tech stacks, you have to start over from scratch.
c16704f9: The core advantage of Kecare lies in its **Non-intrusive** nature.
2ee84e3c:
> **If you are a front-end developer, you can directly "install" a blog system onto your existing application with almost no concern for the technology stack used by the original project.**  
> You just need to create a `.kecare/` directory in your old project, write the content and rules according to the convention, and Kecare will generate the blog-related pages/data for you based on the rules you define, meow~
6ce998ac:
- Vue / Nuxt
- React / Next
- Svelte / SvelteKit
- Traditional Server-Side Rendering Frameworks (SSR)
- Even just an old project (as long as you can integrate the generated results, it's fine)
915e019c:
**Kecare doesn't force you to migrate, change your architecture, or rewrite your pages.**  
It's like an "external content production line," attaching the blog system in the lightest way possible meow~
496aa1b8: ## Two Ways of Usage: Default Theme vs Custom Theme
7f0c761c: Kecare offers two "user-friendly starting paths" for external users, and you can choose according to your needs~
566d4a35: ### 1) Default Theme (Works Out of the Box Meow)
389f739a: If you just want to have a blog that works, looks good, and is ready for writing right away—you can directly use the **default theme** I provide.
e591739b:
- Tech Stack: **Nuxt + Vue3**
- Target Audience: You who want to start writing quickly and get the blog up and running first, meow~
- Features: You almost don't need to understand the internal details of Kecare. Just place the `.kecare/` content as per the documentation and run the commands.
ba65cfc5: If you value the freedom of being "technology stack agnostic," then Kecare's custom theme path is just for you~
54ab04b0:
- Add a `.kecare/` directory in any tech stack/framework (Vue/React/Svelte/SSR/legacy projects)
- Define generation rules in your preferred way (e.g., how articles are converted to HTML, how routing data is generated, how menu data is generated, etc.)
- Let Kecare output consumable content artifacts for your project (such as structured data, static page fragments, resource indexes, etc.)
e28fcb9e: Then your project only needs to do one thing:
45c1d5e8: > **By integrating Kecare's output for rendering (or directly publishing it), you complete a "plugin-style blog system."**
ed8057e8:
This is the ideal state of Kecare:
**Blogging capabilities are additional, portable, and not bound to themes**, meow~
ca1f1ea5: > 💡 **Before You Begin**: If you find any unclear parts in the documentation, feel free to open an Issue or submit a PR to help improve it. Your contribution is greatly appreciated!
b1ef6dea: Kecare is a generator framework designed for modern static blogs and note-taking sites. Its primary goal is not to "create another Hexo," but to decouple the **content system** from the **theme/framework**, turning your blogging capabilities into "pluggable modules."
e24387a0: Many traditional blogging solutions (like Hexo) lock you into a fixed ecosystem: themes, builds, plugins, directory structures are all tightly coupled to it. Switching to a different technology stack often means starting over from scratch.
141ba9d6: The core advantage of Kecare lies in its **non-intrusive** nature.
60aaeaf7: > **One-sentence explanation**: If you are a front-end developer, you can directly "add" a blog system to your existing application, with almost no need to care about the technology stack used by the original project.
3732aef4: You only need to create a `.kecare/` directory in your project and write the content and rules according to the convention. Kecare will then generate blog-related pages and data based on the rules you define.
7acc88c1:
| Type | Framework Examples |
|------|----------|
| Vue Ecosystem | Vue 3, Nuxt |
| React Ecosystem | React, Next.js |
| Svelte Ecosystem | Svelte, SvelteKit |
| Traditional SSR | Any server-side rendering framework |
| Legacy Projects | As long as you can integrate the generated results |
ded0e433: **Kecare doesn't force you to migrate, change your architecture, or rewrite your pages.** It acts like an "external content production line," mounting the blog system into your project in the lightest way possible.
a460fd89: Kecare offers two "friendly getting-started paths" for you to choose from based on your needs:
893b3bcb: If you just want to have a functional, aesthetically pleasing, and writable blog right away, you can directly use the **default theme** provided officially.
9c7f4deb:
| Feature | Description |
|------|------|
| Tech Stack | Nuxt + Vue 3 |
| Target Audience | Users who want to start writing quickly and get their blog up and running |
| Difficulty Level | Low, almost no need to understand Kecare's internal details |
| Startup Method | Place the `.kecare/` content as per the documentation and run the command |
a6cdb801: If you value the freedom of being "technology stack agnostic," the custom theme route is designed for you.
bf160d00:
1. **Add a `.kecare/` directory in any tech stack/framework**
2. **Define generation rules in your preferred way** (e.g., how articles are converted to HTML, how routing data is generated, how menu data is generated, etc.)
3. **Let Kecare output consumable content artifacts for your project** (such as structured data, static page fragments, resource indexes, etc.)
23149b48: Then your project only needs to do one thing:
9a79dcd0: > **Integrating Kecare's output for rendering (or directly publishing) completes a "plug-in blog system".**
ff7be3fe:
- **Blogging capability is additional**: No need to refactor existing projects
- **Portable**: Content is decoupled from themes
- **Theme-agnostic**: Supports any technology stack
d6a6b787: This is the ideal state of Kecare: making the blog system an "add-on module" for your project, rather than a reason to "rewrite everything."
